data.title: Qualcomm 2Q Sales Rise on Automotive, Internet of Things Growth
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Qualcomm logged higher revenue in its latest quarter, boosted by growth in its automotive and internet of things segments, as it contends with pressure from a shortage of memory supply.

The semiconductor company also gave a forecast for the current quarter that came in below Wall Street's expectations.

The stock fell 2.7%, to $151.88, in after-hours trading.

The company posted a fiscal second-quarter profit of $7.37 billion, or $6.88 a share, compared with $2.81 billion, or $2.52 a share, a year earlier. The recent quarter's figure includes a $5.7 billion income tax benefit, or $5.33 per share, from the release of a valuation allowance, the company said.

Adjusted earnings per share were $2.65, compared with estimates of $2.56 a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue rose 3% to $10.6 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $10.59 billion.

The growth was driven by higher sales in the company's automotive and internet of things segments. However, sales in the company's handset segment fell 13%.

Chief Executive Cristiano Amon said the company is navigating a challenging memory environment, and is in the midst of industry-wide transformation amid the rise of AI agents.

The company is also on track to begin initial shipments later this calendar year for a leading hyperscaler, Amon said.

For the current quarter, the company expects adjusted earnings per share of $2.10 to $2.30 and revenue of $9.2 billion to $10 billion. Analysts expect adjusted earnings of $2.42 per share on revenue of $10.19 billion.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Ford Stock Rises on Strong Earnings. Trucks Are a Luxury Good. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Ford Motor smashed earnings expectations. Shares were up in after-hours trading.

Wednesday evening, the auto maker reported a first-quarter operating profit of $3.5 billion from sales of $43.3 billion. Wall Street was looking for an operating profit of $1.3 billion from sales of $42.7 billion.

The operating profit included a $1.3 billion tariff benefit. Backing that out, operating profit was still much better than expected.

Looking ahead, Ford expects 2026 operating profit of $8.5 billion to $10.5 billiion, up from a prior range of $8 billion to $10 billion.

One caution to note: "Guidance does not include potential impacts of a sustained conflict in the Middle East or a significant downturn in the U.S. economy," reads part of the news release.

Shares were up 1% after-hours trading just after results were released.

This is breaking news. Check back for updates. Read a preview of Ford's earnings report below.

Ford Motor has faced some headwinds in 2026. Still, things should be better this year.

The company will report first-quarter numbers after the close of trading on Wednesday. Wall Street is looking for an operating profit of $1.3 billion from sales of $42.7 billion.

A year ago, Ford reported an operating profit of $1 billion from sales of $40.7 billion.

Sales are supposed to rise, but BofA Securities analyst Alexander Perry sees some risk to first-quarter wholesale volumes. (Ford sells cars to dealers and dealers sell to consumers.) "There has been some lumpiness in production from sourcing alternative aluminum hot band material while the Novelis hot mill is down," wrote Perry in a preview report.

Ford has been dealing with aluminum supply issues since a September fire at Novelis' Oswego plant in New York disrupted aluminum production.

Aluminum supply is only one of the things Ford is working through. There are still tariff costs, high oil prices, and commodity price inflation.

General Motors highlighted rising memory chip costs during its first-quarter earnings conference call. Higher memory costs have sent shares of memory chip maker Micron Technology up more than 540% coming into Wednesday trading.

TD Cowen analyst Itay Michaeli expects a strong quarter. Pricing and vehicle mix should overcome many of the headwinds.

Ford expects to generate an operating profit of between $8 billion and $10 billion in 2026. For 2025, Ford reported an operating profit of $6.8 billion, down from $10.2 billion in 2024.

A bump to guidance would help investor sentiment.

GM stock rose 1.3% on Tuesday after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter profits and bumping up 2026 operating profit guidance by $500 million to a range of $13.5 billion to $14.5 billion.

Coming into Wednesday trading, Ford stock was down 5% this year and up 24% over the past 12 months.

Shares were down 1.3% in early trading, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average were off 0.2% and 0.3%, respectively.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Murphy USA Q1 adjusted EBITDA jumps 76%, beats estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. fuel retailer's Q1 adjusted EBITDA rose sharply, beating analyst expectations

  • Net income more than doubled yr/yr, driven by higher fuel contribution margins

  • Company repurchased 169,000 shares for $70.9 mln during the quarter

Outlook

  • Murphy USA expects to open 45-55 new stores in 2026

  • Company says April fuel volumes are roughly flat to prior year

  • Murphy USA expects April all-in fuel margins of 35-40 cpg

Result Drivers

  • FUEL MARGINS - Higher total fuel contribution margins and increased retail fuel volumes drove a 40.6% rise in total fuel contribution dollars

  • NICOTINE SALES - Strong nicotine product sales boosted merchandise margin contribution, offsetting weaker non-nicotine discretionary categories

  • FUEL SUPPLY VOLATILITY - Market-driven pricing effects and timing of inventory movements increased fuel supply contribution, including RINs

data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. oncology biotech's Q1 net loss and operating loss beat analyst expectations

  • Q1 revenue was $1 mln, up from no revenue a year ago, due to a licensing payment

data.title: CVR Energy Q1 sales beat estimates, adjusted loss widens
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. refiner and fertilizer maker's Q1 sales beat analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 missed analyst expectations

  • Company posted net loss due to $158 mln in unrealized derivative losses

Outlook

  • Company expects Q2 2026 petroleum throughput of 200,000-215,000 bpd

  • Company sees Q2 2026 crude utilization between 92% and 99%

  • Company expects Q2 2026 nitrogen fertilizer ammonia utilization rate of 95%-100%

Result Drivers

  • DERIVATIVE LOSSES - Q1 net loss included $158 mln in unrealized derivative losses, which do not reflect $447 mln in expected value from NYMEX crack spread swaps to be realized through 2027

  • STRONG FERTILIZER DEMAND - Nitrogen Fertilizer segment saw higher net income, supported by robust demand for the spring planting season and higher ammonia and UAN pricing

  • HIGH UTILIZATION RATES - Company reported crude utilization of 97% and ammonia plant utilization of 103%, indicating solid operational performance

data.title: Alphabet Gets Lone Post Earnings Bump Among 'Mag 7' Cos Reporting — Market Talk
data.description: With four of the "Magnificent 7" reporting earnings after the bell, Alphabet alone seems to be doing enough to please investors. Shares of Google's parent company rise 3.9% after results beat expectations, with CEO Sundar Pichai unsurprisingly crediting AI investments underpinning much of the growth across the company. Amazon.com, Meta Platforms and Microsoft all also post robust growth, but shares are down out of the gate, with Meta down more than 5% The Facebook owner forecast sales for its current quarter that's in-line with expectation and also bumped up its capex plans for the year, reflecting higher prices for components and, to a lesser extent, additional data center costs. (paul.ziobro@wsj.com)
